findings.evidence: Diff lines 286-299 define `prodFst`, `prodSnd`, `prodInl`, `prodInr`; lines 302-318 define `biprod`, `biprodFst`, `biprodSnd`, `biprodInl`, `biprodInr` as aliases. Mathlib names this interface `biprod.fst`, `biprod.snd`, `biprod.inl`, `biprod.inr` in `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/Shapes/BinaryBiproducts.lean:482-502`.
findings.fix: Keep the four-map interface under biproduct terminology only, or prove/provide the standard categorical biproduct API; do not expose `prodInl`/`prodInr` as product-named bundled morphisms.
findings.issue: `ComoduleCat.prodFst`/`prodSnd`/`prodInl`/`prodInr` expose a biproduct-style four-map API under product names, while lines 302-318 add duplicate `biprod*` aliases for the same maps.

summary: The bundled API still mixes product and biproduct naming for the same four-map construction. That makes the categorical interface misleading and duplicates the public names that should be reserved for biproduct terminology.
